name: mill-grain
summary: A grain mill built around a massive turning millstone. Grain sacks stacked high, flour dust in the air, a hopper above the stone. Often water- or wind-driven with shafts disappearing into a wall.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: squareish, the millstone dictates the center

primary_palette.note: single-stone mill — stone dominates the center, hopper above
water-wheel mill — drive shaft emerges from one wall

story_prompts: harvest-week peak — every cell stacked with grain sacks, miller and two apprentices covered in flour
stone-dressing day — millstone lifted, chisels out, operation halted
flood-broken — water wheel shaft cracked, stone silent, miller arguing with creditors
night watch — dust settled, one lantern, a thief picking the scales

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: dusty working light (0.35-0.45) with sunbeams through high windows

anti_patterns: millstone belongs centered — it's the machine the whole room serves
grain sacks cluster along walls, never block the working path to the stone
avoid open flames near flour dust — brazier must be far from working zone or implied doused
